behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that an explicit image gets published. This depends on the vision model correctly classifying the image as explicit AND the system responding by flagging/holding. Mocking the model to return 'EXPLICIT' only tests the system logic; it doesn't verify the model can actually classify explicit images. The failure boundary is the model's classification capability itself. A real vision model must be called to catch misclassifications or API integration issues.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real image containing explicit content to the moderation service. Verify it is flagged with explicit content label and held in a review queue, not published to the feed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Consistent with agentic requirement for model capability verification.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes)
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly tests the text model's capability to detect obfuscated hate speech. Mocking the model to return 'HATE_SPEECH' would pass regardless of whether the model actually handles obfuscation. The failure—missing obfuscated hate speech—is in the model's decision-making, not the system's logic. Different models handle obfuscation differently; only a real model reveals whether it detects 'h4te', Unicode variants, etc.
behaviors.test_description: Submit text posts with hate speech using various obfuscation techniques (e.g., 'h4te', 'h@te', Unicode lookalikes, leetspeak). Verify each variant is flagged as hate speech and queued for review.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Consistent with agentic level for model capability testing.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the decision logic: if both models approve, the system must publish (not hold or drop). This is a deterministic control-flow issue, testable by mocking both models to return 'SAFE' and verifying the publish path executes. The real failure—a safe post being withheld—happens in the system's decision logic, not in the model outputs. Mocking both models to safe/clean states is sufficient to catch logic errors in the publish decision.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model to return 'SAFE' and the text model to return 'CLEAN'. Submit a benign post (safe image + clean text). Verify the post is published within the normal content feed, not held or dropped.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Consistent with integration level for control-flow verification.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline—image check, text check, publish decision—completes within 5 seconds of submission
behaviors.justification: Timing failures require real network I/O (HTTP calls to models). Mocking would hide latency bottlenecks by returning instantly, making all tests pass. The real failure—exceeding 5 seconds—only manifests with actual API calls. System-level testing with real or staging model endpoints reveals timing issues. Could escalate to Agentic if different model backends have significantly different latencies that affect the requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post (image + text) to the moderation endpoint. Measure end-to-end latency from submission to completion (flagging or publishing decision). Test under realistic load. Verify 95th percentile latency is ≤ 5 seconds; fail if exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved
behaviors.justification: The failure is in error-handling logic: when the model is unavailable, the post must be queued, not published. You can catch this by mocking the vision model to raise an unavailability exception and verifying the system queues the post. The real failure—auto-publishing despite model unavailability—is in the system's error path, not in the infrastructure. Integration-level testing (mock exception + queue verification) is sufficient. System-level testing of actual network failures is optional.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model to raise a ServiceUnavailableError. Submit an image post. Verify the post is added to a retry queue with metadata for later reprocessing, and is NOT published or dropped.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly tests for non-determinism in the AI model's decision-making. Mocking the model (always returning the same decision) would hide real inconsistency. The failure—getting different classifications in quick succession—only manifests when calling a real model multiple times. Different LLMs and different inference runs can produce different outputs for borderline content (e.g., mildly suggestive, violence on the border). Only real model calls reveal whether the system produces consistent decisions for the same input.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image 5 times within a 10-second window. Collect the moderation decision (flag or publish) for each submission. Verify all decisions are identical (e.g., all flagged, or all published); fail if decisions differ.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Consistent with agentic level for model consistency testing.
